arts.ttl: LIVE West Brom v Portsmouth: build-up, team news and live updates from The Hawthorns
arts.sum: Pompey go in search of their third win on the bounce as they take on West Brom today.

arts.ttl: Portsmouth boss details emergency left-back plans after surprise transfer stance
arts.sum: John Mousinsho has outlined his back-up plans at left-back after stating Pompey are not actively looking for a replacement for Jacob Farrell.

arts.ttl: Portsmouth rivals Derby leading race for huge former Fratton favourite as Championship relegation fears grow
arts.sum: Pompey relegation rivals Derby County are reportedly set to turn to a huge former Fratton favourite to help stop their slide down the Championship table.

arts.ttl: Luton Town in ‘advanced talks’, player can leave ‘quickly’ – Want to get ahead of Norwich and West Brom
arts.sum: Luton Town, Norwich City and West Bromwich Albion are among the clubs in the picture to sign FC St. Gallen star Willem Geubbels in the winter market. That’s according to Foot Mercato, who tag Norwich City and West Brom when covering the latest on Luton Town. arts.ttl: 'Have got to blame someone' - Sammy Silvera speaks for first time after Portsmouth boss was told to 'send him back' to Middlesbrough
arts.sum: Sammy Silvera has spoken for the first time about his struggles at Pompey.

arts.ttl: Saturday's Championship predictions including West Bromwich Albion vs. Portsmouth

arts.ttl: West Brom v Portsmouth injury and team news as 8 out and 2 doubts
arts.sum: Pompey and West Brom clash in the EFL Championship on Saturday, January 25.

arts.ttl: Racic, Diakite, Frabotta, Cole - Tony Mowbray's instruction to West Brom's fringe players - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Mowbray, who is already looking forward to next week when he'll have a full week on the training pitch without the distraction of a midweek fixture, is slowly doing his homework and keeping a keen eye on those on the fringes of the starting XI as things stand so that, when he needs to change things from the bench, he knows what he has at his disposal. Albion went abroad in the summer and filled their quota of overseas players who they were eligible to sign through the relevant criteria, but Mowbray wouldn't be as well versed in the qualities and performance levels of the likes of Ousmane Diakite, Uros Racic and Gianluca Frabotta.

arts.ttl: ‘Portsmouth told me I was staying, releasing me was worst day of my life': the dark side of the football dream
arts.sum: All Harry Kavanagh had ever wanted to do was play for Pompey.
